title: Document Database Example (MongoDB)
description: Example showing flexible schema in MongoDB document database
explanation: MongoDB stores documents in BSON format. Documents in the same collection can have different fields, demonstrating flexible schema. Document databases allow nested objects and arrays.

title: Key-Value Store Operations
description: Basic operations in a key-value store like Redis
explanation: Key-value stores provide simple get/put operations. Values can be strings, lists, sets, or hashes. Excellent for caching, session storage, and simple lookups.

title: Column-Family Store Example (Cassandra)
description: Creating column families in Apache Cassandra
explanation: Column-family stores like Cassandra use wide rows. Each row can have millions of columns. Data is organized by row key and column name, optimized for read/write of large datasets.

complexity.time: O(1) for key lookup, O(n) for range scans

title: Graph Database Example (Neo4j)
description: Creating nodes and relationships in Neo4j graph database
code:
// GRAPH DATABASE (NEO4J) - Cypher Query Language

// Create nodes
CREATE (john:Person {name: 'John', age: 25})
CREATE (jane:Person {name: 'Jane', age: 28})
CREATE (bob:Person {name: 'Bob', age: 30})
CREATE (google:Company {name: 'Google'})

// Create relationships
CREATE (john)-[:WORKS_AT {since: 2020}]->(google)
CREATE (jane)-[:WORKS_AT {since: 2019}]->(google)
CREATE (john)-[:FRIENDS_WITH]->(jane)
CREATE (john)-[:FRIENDS_WITH]->(bob)

// Find all friends of John
MATCH (john:Person {name: 'John'})-[:FRIENDS_WITH]->(friend)
RETURN friend.name

// Find friends who work at Google
MATCH (john:Person {name: 'John'})-[:FRIENDS_WITH]->(friend)
MATCH (friend)-[:WORKS_AT]->(company:Company)
WHERE company.name = 'Google'
RETURN friend.name, company.name

// Count John's friends
MATCH (john:Person {name: 'John'})-[:FRIENDS_WITH]->(friend)
RETURN count(friend) as friend_count
explanation: Graph databases use nodes (entities) and edges (relationships). Relationships are first-class citizens with properties. Ideal for social networks, recommendation engines, fraud detection.

complexity.time: O(1) for indexed lookup, O(depth) for traversal
